data.title: Roblox shares at an 'excellent entry point' after recent downturn - Oppenheimer
data.description:
Analysts at Oppenheimer upgraded Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) to "outperform" froma previous investment rating of "market perform," as the recent fall in share prices opened up "an excellent entry point" for those who wish to hold on for long.

"Roblox is one of the most exciting profitable growth stories in the interactive entertainment industry. Recent pullback in the stock and a business that's insulated from tariff discussion, in our view, create an excellent entry opportunity for long-term investors," Oppenheimer said Thursday.

The research firm's recent checks indicated that revenue contribution from programmatic video ads will materialize in 2025 for the online gaming company.

"Longer term, we believe RBLX is well positioned to sustain bookings growth and margin expansion at rates that outperform the video game industry, based on its unique user growth momentum, technology infrastructure, and untapped monetization potential," Oppenheimer added.

RBLX has a PT of $70, implying an upside of 22.1%. Shares of the company are down 2.5% YTD, while the benchmark S&P index is down over 7%.

data.title: Federal budget deficit narrows less than expected in March
Novartis is reportedly eyeing $23B investment in U.S.
data.description:
• Novartis (NYSE:NVS) announced Thursday it plans to invest $23B to build and expand 10 U.S. plants amid President Donald Trump’s repeated threats to impose tariffs on pharmaceutical goods, Reuters reported.
  • As part of a five-year plan, the Swiss drugmaker is looking to build a new R&D facility in San Diego, California, in addition to six new manufacturing sites, some of which are tasked with producing raw pharmaceutical ingredients.
  • The expansion is anticipated to generate over 10,000 skilled jobs for those such as engineers and scientists, while another 3,000 will benefit from newly created supporting roles and construction jobs. The company has yet to decide on the locations of the new sites.
  • Amid President Trump’s oft-stated plans to impose pharmaceutical tariffs, leading drugmakers such as Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Eli Lilly (LLY), and Merck (MRK) have announced major U.S. investments.

data.title: No protests and a lot of oil: Tesla finally launches in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Despite cheap gas and a dearth of charging stations, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) will begin selling cars in the oil-rich nation of Saudi Arabia, holding a launch event in Riyadh today before opening up for business on Friday.

“Explore our global bestselling lineup and step into a world powered by solar energy, sustained by batteries, and driven by electric vehicles,” the company said in a statement on its website announcing the launch.

Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) introduction into the Saudi market comes as the country’s Vision 2030 initiative sets a goal of sourcing at least 50% of its power from renewable energy and transitioning 30% of Saudi vehicles to electric by 2030 in an effort to diversify its oil-based economy and lower emissions in the capital city.

As part of this push, other EV manufacturers from across the globe have already established a foothold in the Saudi auto market, including California-based Lucid Motors (LCID) and China’s BYD (BYDDF, BYDDY). The kingdom is also developing its own EV brand called Ceer Motors (Arabic for “drive forward”) through a joint venture between the Saudi sovereign wealth fund and Taiwan’s Foxconn. The company plans to begin distribution across the Middle East this year.

Protest-Free environment

One unexpected Tesla perk in Saudi Arabia: zero outrage over Elon’s DOGE affiliation.

Recent U.S. sales data highlight growing consumer pushback against Musk’s campaign to curb government fraud and waste.

“Tesla has essentially become a political symbol globally....and that is a very bad thing for the future of this disruptive tech stalwart and the brand crisis tornado that has now turned into an F5 tornado," said Wedbush analyst Dan Ives, who estimates that Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has lost 10% of its future customer global base due to “self-created brand issues” with that number as high as 20% in Europe.

Added J.P. Morgan’s Ryan Brinkman, the trend is sales is worse than appreciated with “unprecedented” brand damage.

Musk’s public image is not blemish-free in Saudi Arabia, however, after very public social media feuds with high-ranking Saudi officials, and a failed attempt to take Tesla (TSLA) private with Saudi backing. Because of his fraught relationship with members of the Saudi royal family, Musk established markets in surrounding nations like the UAE, Jordan, and Qatar, and steered clear of Saudi Arabia until now.

data.title: MBX Biosciences initiated at Market Outperform by Citizens
data.description:
• MBX Biosciences (NASDAQ:MBX) was initiated at Market Outperform by Citizens banking on the company’s compelling PEP platform and strategic focus.
  • Citizens believes the company's method of targeting clinically validated pathways in indications with clear regulatory paths and significant unmet medical needs increases the likelihood of clinical and commercial success. 
  • Citizens has initiated MBX at $38 risk-adjusted, DCF-derived price target. 
  • MBX has fallen 66% YTD.

data.title: Energy stocks sink alongside crude oil as traders focus on escalating U.S.-China trade war
data.description:
Energy (NYSEARCA:XLE) shows the widest loss with all 11 S&P stock sectors trading deeply in the red on Thursday, a day after the price of oil bounced off levels last seen in February 2021.

With global markets in turmoil, President Trump announced a 90-day halt on higher tariffs against most trading partners, but investor focus has shited to the deepening trade war between the U.S. and China after Trump raised duties on the country to 125%.

China is the world's largest oil importer, and the higher U.S. tariffs may weigh on the consumption of fuels and petrochemicals; even before Trump returned to the White House, China's usage of gasoline and diesel had been contracting, in part because of the country's sustained property crisis, and in part because of the spread of electric vehicles and renewables.

U.S. WTI front-month crude oil (CL1:COM) for May delivery -4.9% to $59.26/bbl and front-month June Brent crude (CO1:COM) -4.5% to $62.52/bbl, giving up all of the previous session's sharp gains.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:XLE), (NYSEARCA:XOP), (AMLP), (VDE), (OIH), (IXC), (CRAK)

APA Corp. (APA) is the sector's weakest performer, -14.2%, giving back the bulk of yesterday's gain; among other major oil and gas names, Devon Energy (DVN) -12.2%, SLB (SLB) -10.9%, ConocoPhillips (COP) -10%, Occidental Petroleum (OXY) -10%, Diamondback Energy (FANG) -9.6%, EOG Resources (EOG) -9.4%, Marathon Petroleum (MPC) -8.9%, Phillips 66 (PSX) -8.2%, Valero Enegy (VLO) -7.7%, Chevron (CVX) -7.3%, Exxon Mobil (XOM) -6.5%, Oneok (OKE) -6%.

Higher tariffs against China likely will cut U.S. crude exports to the country, backing up supply and raising U.S. storage levels, Ritterbusch analysts said Thursday, and U.S. crude oil exports to China fell to 112K bbl/day in March, 40% below last year's 190K bbl/day, according to data from vessel tracker Kpler.

data.title: US needs ‘brief recession’ to rebuild its foundation says Kyle Bass
data.description:
The U.S. may have to make “difficult choices” and go into “a brief recession” to rebuild its foundation, according to Kyle Bass, founder of Conservation Equity Management.

The investor and founder of the Texas-based private equity firm that focuses on environmental sustainability told Bloomberg that the U.S. may need to undergo a brief recession to reorganize its global trade ties and fix its federal budget deficit. According to him, short-term pain may be necessary for the U.S. economy to become stronger.

“We have to make some difficult choices,” he said during a Bloomberg interview. “[Those choices] might be slightly recessionary, and if that’s true, we might have to go to a brief recession in order to rebuild our foundation.”

President Donald Trump’s tariff impositions have caused U.S. stocks to plummet. The S&P 500 (SP500) is down 11.67% year-to-date, and down 15.4% from its Feb. 19 peak. Similarly, the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) is down 16.16% year-to-date, and down 19.3% from its peak.

The 30-year Treasury yield had its largest three-day increase since the pandemic, rising 0.072 percentage points; and the 10-year yield’s three-day gain was the biggest since the summer of 2022, rising 0.151 points on Wednesday.

“This is a necessary resetting so that we can grow again in the future, and grow more profitably,” Bass said.

“If we’re doing tit-for-tat on tariffs, China loses, because there is no functional equivalency in the amount of goods and services moving back and forth. We do expect the Chinese currency to weaken pretty materially.”

data.title: Thinking Machines Lab seeking to raise $2B at near $10B valuation: report
data.description:
Thinking Machines Lab, the artificial intelligence startup founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, is in the process of raising $2B in funding, Business Insider reported.

That amount is double what the company was seeking less than two months ago, the news outlet added. It's not clear exactly what valuation Thinking Machines Lab would get, but two people familiar with the matter told the news outlet it is at least $10B.

Murati, who was at Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI for more than six years, started Thinking Machines Lab in February with three premises: helping people adapt AI systems to work for their specific needs; developing strong foundations to build more capable AI systems; and fostering a culture of open science that helps the whole field understand and improve these systems.

In addition to Murati, former OpenAI executives at Thinking Machines Lab include John Schulman, who co-founded the ChatGPT maker in 2015 and Barret Zoph. Schulman will be Thinking Machine's chief scientist, while Zoph, who also left OpenAI in September, will be its CTO.

Several other former OpenAI employees are listed on Thinking Machine's website, as are former Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Meta (META) employees.

Safe Superintelligence, an AI startup from former OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever, raised more than $1B in new funding that valued the company at $30B in February.

data.title: Top 15 SA ranked China stocks as Beijing and Washington’s trade war heats up
data.description:
President Donald Trump provided a temporary reprieve for markets by announcing a 90-day pause on planned reciprocal tariffs, offering relief to some trading partners on Wednesday.

However, China was not among them, as Beijing received even higher tariffs. In a move that signals rising tensions in the ongoing trade war between the world’s two largest economies, the U.S. imposed a sharp increase in tariffs on Chinese imports—raising them to 125%. 

“Based on the lack of respect that China has shown to the World’s Markets, I am hereby raising the Tariff charged to China by the United States of America to 125%, effective immediately,” Trump said on Truth Social. 

As a result, Seeking Alpha highlighted the top 15 China ADRs from a market cap perspective then ranked the list according to its quant metrics. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

No. 15: DiDi Global (OTCPK:DIDIY), quant grade of 2.68.

No. 14: Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI), quant grade of 3.02.

data.title: S&P 500 rally stalls at key resistance, limited further upside - BTIG
data.description:
BTIG noted on Wednesday that the S&P 500 (SP500) has rebounded to nearly the halfway mark between its all-time high and its recent April low, suggesting a potential pause in the current rally. While the firm acknowledges the possibility of further short-term gains, it does not anticipate a sustained move higher beyond this retracement level.

“SPX is nearly at its 50% retracement of the entire decline (5491). There is an unfilled gap at 5571 which we think can get filled in coming days, but don't see meaningful near-term upside beyond that,” BTIG’s Jonathan Krinsky said. 

As of Thursday morning, the S&P 500 was trading at 5,456.89, reflecting a 12.8% rebound from its 2025 low of 4,835.04 reached on April 7. However, the index remains well below its record high of 6,147.43 set on February 19 of this year as selling activity picks back up. 

Despite the recent recovery, the benchmark average continues to trade beneath its key 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving averages, indicating that broader technical pressures remain in place. 

For investors looking to track the overall movement of the S&P 500 (SP500), several index ETFs and mutual funds are worth considering, including: 

S&P 500 Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).
